pages.raw_text:
! To the Editor of The Press:


have been an interested spectator


of this free speech fight from the


beginning and have an observation
| or two to express on this day of
thanksgiving. / have noted the
brutalities of some of the police,
the apathy of the general public,
and the malicious and distorted ac-
counts of the whole affair that have
been given. to the public in the
Review and the Chronicle. Note
in last Sundays Review under the
caption. why Spokane Should be
Thankful" Prominent Citizens See
Numerous Spiritual and Mental
Blessings to Call Forth Gratitude
Next Thursday" THE General Up
lift Movement has put it's Benedic-
ton Upon the Inland Empire"


There is much rejoicing among
these prominent citizens" over ma
terial prosperity. l searched earn
estly for one breath of a broad,
cosmic spirit; one suggestion of a
social consciousness which recog-
nized the poor and lowly, over all
the world, as well as the prosper
Gus and well fed. True, some senti-
mental clap trap was INDUCED in,
such as noble sentiment" "univer-
sal peace" principles of free man.
hood" charity and Kindness" and
all the while these prominent citi-
zens," who are evidently suffering
from "the illusion of the near"
seem not to realize that here in our
city over 300 men are imprisoned,
suffering from slow starvation, sick
and manned by brutal handling of
the police. Where, oh where is the
general uplift movement" I have
no patience with the inane utter
ances of the minister who pretends
to be follower of the lowly Naza-
rene and yet rejoices and thanks
God for city government which
"has an arm long enough to reach
out and throttle" minister
preaching long drawn out sermons
on Love Thy Neighbor" and THE
General Uplift Movement" l am
exasperated at the comfortable
thoughts of these well-fed men, at
their Indifference to human suffer
ing in their midst, at their high
sounding, empty phrases and lack
of clear thinking.


On the evening of the day the
first six installments of l. w. W..
